body: I think this might be all we need-- it won't be efficient, but I don't think it needs to be since it happens at initialization
title: Try to fix lr hydrostatic balance func for gpus

body:
<!--- THESE LINES ARE COMMENTED -->
## Purpose 
Closes #2497.

The main change is that in a deep atmosphere, the coriolis vector now aligns with the rotational axis, which means the contravariant12 components are no longer zero. I've added the extra terms (which appear naturally on the faces.

It requires https://github.com/CliMA/ClimaCore.jl/pull/1591.

This PR also updates the dependencies to use ClimaCore@0.12, which enables grid construction on GPUs with surface warping. 

## To-do

- Proposed task

body:
This PR hoists and precomputes `ᶜgradᵥ_θ_virt,ᶜgradᵥ_q_tot,ᶜgradᵥ_θ_liq_ice`, to simplify some very expensive kernels. A step towards #2530.

Let's see how the flame graph updates with this PR.

The flame graphs show an improvement with the kernel but, more interestingly, the gradient-interpolations are pretty expensive. Since this PR hoists those computations, this PR should be more performant since those computations are repeated in the main branch. We should probably look into the performance of the gradient-interpolations, but we can also merge this in case we can't improve it as much as we hoped.
title: Hoist and precompute common variables

body: This PR reduces the number of fields in `EnvBuoyGradVars` in order to reduce the loads/stores in the buoyancy kernel. This should be better on gpus, too.
title: Shrink EnvBuoyGradVars
